Agent.description: The agent system a [`Trajectory`] was produced by (ATIF _AgentSchema_).

extra.description: Custom agent configuration not covered by the core schema.

model_name.description: Default LLM for the trajectory; a step-level `model_name` overrides it.

tool_definitions.description:
Tool/function definitions available to the agent (OpenAI function
schema). Opaque to Mira — carried, never introspected.

AxisInfo.description:
One extra matrix axis advertised by `list`, so the host can plan the full
cross-product without running anything.

CancelParams.description:
`cancel` params: the `id` of the in-flight `run`/`execute`/`score` [`Request`]
to abort. It is the request's own `id` (the one the host assigned and awaits a
response on), not a case key — so a host can cancel a specific outstanding call
even when several runs of the same case are in flight.

CancelResult.description:
`cancel` result: whether a matching in-flight request was found and aborted.
`false` is normal and benign — the targeted request had already completed (or
was never in flight) by the time the cancel arrived. Cancellation is therefore
best-effort: a `run` that finishes first still returns its real result.

ContentPart.description:
One multimodal content piece (ATIF _ContentPartSchema_, v1.6+): `type` is
`"text"` (with `text` set) or `"image"` (with `source` set). Kept an open
struct — not a tagged enum — so a future ATIF content type parses instead
of failing.

ErrorKind.description:
Why a run failed, when it did — set alongside [`Transcript::error`].

A [`Subject`](ErrorKind::Subject) error is the model/agent *under test*
getting it wrong: it ran but crashed on the input, produced garbage, or blew
its turn budget — a real failure the eval should catch. An
[`Infra`](ErrorKind::Infra) error is the scaffolding *around* the run breaking
(out of budget/quota, rate-limited, a provider 5xx/outage, a network/timeout
fault): not the model's fault. Infra failures are surfaced as **N/A**
([`Score::na`]) so they are excluded from the case verdict and aggregate
(neither pass nor fail, like [`Score::na`] for a single scorer), and the host
retries them up to `--max-retries`. See [`Transcript::infra_error`].

oneOf.description: The subject/model under test errored: a real, scoreable failure.

The infrastructure around the run errored: not the model's fault, scored
N/A (not failed), and retryable.

EvalInfo.description:
One eval, as advertised by `list`. Enough for the host to plan the full
`samples × targets` grid and apply selection without running anything.

axes.description:
Extra matrix axes beyond the target. Defaulted so older servers that omit
the field still parse (forward compatibility).

max_turns.description:
Defaulted so a foreign/older study that omits it still parses, per the
protocol's forward-compatibility contract (see docs/protocol.md).

next_cursor.description:
Opaque continuation token: present iff more samples remain beyond
`samples`. Pass it back via `list_samples` to fetch the next page. The
host treats it as an opaque blob — only the study interprets it.
Defaulted/omitted, so an older study that sends all samples inline (and a
host that ignores pagination) interoperate unchanged.

samples.description:
The **first page** of this eval's samples. When `next_cursor` is present,
more pages follow — fetch them with `list_samples` (see
[`ListSamplesParams`]) until the cursor runs out. A study that fits its
whole dataset here omits `next_cursor`.

seed.description:
Base seed the study declared for reproducible trials (trial `t` uses
`seed + t`). The host threads it into runs unless `--seed` overrides.
Defaulted/omitted when the study left seeding to the subject.

trials.description:
How many times each case of this eval should be run (trials/repetitions),
for pass@k / variance over a stochastic subject. `0`/`1` mean a single
run (no trial dimension). The host may override with `--trials`. Defaulted
so older/foreign studies that omit it still parse.

EventParams.description:
Typed payload of an `event` progress [`Notification`] for one in-flight run.

Correlated to its originating `run`/`execute` request by `request_id` — the
same demultiplexing key responses use — so a host can bind progress to a
specific call even when many cases (including repeated trials of one case)
are multiplexed over the single pipe.

kind.description:
One of the [`event`] kinds. A future kind an older host doesn't recognise
is carried through verbatim (forward-compat), not rejected.

params.description: Extra matrix-axis values for the case (empty for a target-only matrix).

request_id.description:
The `id` of the `run`/`execute` request this event belongs to. Defaulted
to 0 ("uncorrelated") so a study that omits it still parses.

text.description: Streamed output delta, for an [`event::OUTPUT`] event.

ExecuteResult.description:
`execute` result for one case: the **full** [`Transcript`] (raw events and
captured files included), so the host can persist it as an execution
artifact and `score` it later. Distinct from [`RunResult`], which carries the
lightweight [`TranscriptSummary`] plus scores.

params.description: Extra matrix-axis values for this case (empty for a target-only matrix).

seed.description: Per-trial seed this transcript was produced with, when set.

skipped.description: True when the case was not executed (e.g. target unavailable).

transcript.description: The complete transcript, unlike the summary carried in [`RunResult`].

trial.description: 0-based trial index when this case is repeated (see [`RunParams::trial`]).

trials.description:
Total trials for this case (`0`/`1` = single run). Part of the case key
when `> 1`, so trial artifacts stay distinct.

extra.description:
Custom aggregate metrics (`reasoning_tokens` is read from here by the
usage projection when present).

total_cost_usd.description: Total cost for the whole trajectory, in USD.

total_steps.description: Total steps (may differ from `steps.len()` when explained in `notes`).

ImageSource.description:
An image stored alongside the trajectory and referenced by path/URL (ATIF
_ImageSourceSchema_) — never embedded bytes.

path.description:
Relative or absolute file path, or a URL (conventionally an `images/`
sidecar next to the trajectory file).

capabilities.description:
Named capabilities this study supports beyond the base methods, so hosts
can feature-detect additively (e.g. `"axes"`, `"events"`). Defaulted, so
an older study that omits it is treated as base-only.

capability_params.description:
Structured **config** for capabilities, keyed by capability token — the
data a bare [`capabilities`](Self::capabilities) string can't carry (which
event kinds the study emits, the input/output modalities it supports, a
concurrency hint, …). Open-vocabulary like `metadata`, so new keys need no
version bump; a host reads it additively, falling back to today's
behaviour when a token is absent.

study_version.description:
Optional study version string (e.g. the study crate's version). For
diagnostics; defaulted for forward/backward compatibility.

ListSamplesParams.description:
`list_samples` params: ask for one more page of `eval`'s samples, continuing
from the opaque `cursor` last handed back (in [`EvalInfo::next_cursor`] or a
prior [`ListSamplesResult::next_cursor`]). Only studies advertising the
`paginate` capability answer this.

cursor.description: Opaque token from the previous page. The host echoes it verbatim.

ListSamplesResult.description:
`list_samples` result: one page of samples plus the cursor for the page after
it (`None` once the dataset is exhausted).

next_cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page, or `None` when this was the last page.

LogParams.description: Typed payload of a `log` [`Notification`] — a free-form diagnostic line.

request_id.description:
The request this log relates to (0 for connection-level logs). Same
`#[serde(default)]` treatment as `EventParams::request_id`, so both carry a
consistent `default: 0` in the generated schema (no `skip_serializing_if`,
which would suppress the schema default and desync SDK generators).

Notification.description:
study → host, fire-and-forget progress (no `id`). Carries live events (a
turn started, a tool was called, tokens spent) so the host can render
progress and, later, stream into a transcript viewer.

results.description: One result per tool call or action.

content.description:
The tool/action output (text or multimodal parts). May be omitted when
`subagent_trajectory_ref` carries the full detail.

extra.description: Custom result-level metadata (retrieval score, source doc id, …).

source_call_id.description:
The [`ToolCall::tool_call_id`] this result answers; absent for actions
outside the standard tool-calling format.

subagent_trajectory_ref.description:
References to delegated subagent trajectories (embedded via
`trajectory_id` or external via `trajectory_path`).

Part.description:
One piece of multimodal content. The discriminant serializes as a `kind`
tag (`text` / `image` / `audio` / `file` / `json`), so a part is a single
self-describing JSON object. Media variants reference their bytes through a
[`Source`] (exactly one of `uri` / `data`).

oneOf.description:
Plain text — the same content `final_response` / `input` carry, but as a
part so it can sit alongside media in one ordered list.

An arbitrary file (a document, an archive, …), with an optional display
`name`.

A structured JSON value — for tool-style / structured outputs that aren't
free text and aren't a media blob.

Response.description: study → host, correlated to a [`Request`] by `id`.

RpcError.description:
A structured, JSON-RPC-shaped protocol-level error.

Distinct from a transcript's `error`/`error_kind`, which classify a *subject*
failure (the target under test got it wrong). An [`RpcError`] is the failure of
the RPC itself — bad params, an unknown method, a study-side crash, a provider
outage surfaced at the transport. `code` and `retryable` let the host classify
and retry the request **without parsing the human `message`**, and `data`
carries optional structured context.

All fields beyond `message` are optional and defaulted, so a peer that sends
bare `{ "message": "…" }` still parses (forward/backward compat).

code.description:
Numeric class of the failure (JSON-RPC convention; see [`codes`]). `0`
when unclassified. Defaulted so older peers that omit it still parse.

data.description:
Optional structured payload for programmatic handling (JSON-RPC `data`).
Omitted from the wire when absent.

message.description: Human-readable description. The only required field.

retryable.description:
Hint that retrying the identical request may succeed — a *transient
infrastructure* fault (provider outage, timeout, rate limit), not the
caller's mistake. Defaulted `false` so older peers parse and unknown
failures aren't retried blindly.

RunParams.description:
`run` params: address one matrix case by `(eval, sample, target label)` plus
any extra axis `params` (axis name → chosen value).

params.description:
Chosen value per extra matrix axis. Empty/omitted for a target-only
matrix; defaulted so older hosts/servers interoperate.

seed.description:
Per-trial seed for reproducibility, when the host set one. The study
threads it to the subject (see [`crate::Trial::seed`]).

trial.description:
0-based trial index when this case is being repeated; `0` for a single
run. Defaulted so older hosts/studies interoperate.

trials.description:
Total trials planned for this case (`0`/`1` = single run). Lets the study
echo the case's trial identity back so its key matches the host's plan.

RunResult.description: `run` result for one case. Also the unit persisted in checkpoints.

expected.description: The sample's expected/reference value, when the dataset provides one.

input.description:
The sample's input turns (the prompt sent), copied through so a persisted
result is self-describing without the dataset. Empty when unavailable
(e.g. a synthetic error result).

params.description: Extra matrix-axis values for this case (empty for a target-only matrix).

seed.description: Per-trial seed this result was produced with, when set.

skipped.description: True when the case was not executed (e.g. target unavailable).

trial.description: 0-based trial index when this case is repeated (see [`RunParams::trial`]).

trials.description:
Total trials for this case (`0`/`1` = single run). Part of the case key
when `> 1`; the host groups results by their *logical* key (without the
trial suffix) to aggregate pass@k / variance (see [`crate::aggregate`]).

metadata.description:
Free-form sample provenance (repo, difficulty, dataset split, …). Mirrors
`Sample::metadata` onto the wire so the host can group reports by it
(`--group-by`). Defaulted/omitted when empty, so an older study still parses.

Score.description:
Outcome of a single [`Scorer`] on a [`Transcript`].

`value` is a continuous score in `0.0..=1.0`; `pass` is the boolean verdict
(often `value >= threshold`). Keeping both lets a scorer report a graded
signal while still contributing a pass/fail to the matrix.

A third state — **N/A** ([`na`](Score::na)) — lets a scorer say "I couldn't
evaluate this" (an unreachable judge, a missing API key, any infra hiccup)
rather than crashing the run or lying with a `fail`. An N/A score is excluded
from the case verdict and the aggregate: it neither passes nor fails.

na.description:
True when the scorer did not apply / could not run (infra issue, missing
credentials, …). Excluded from the case verdict and aggregate.

ScoreParams.description:
`score` params: a case identity plus the full [`Transcript`] to score. The
transcript travels over the wire so the host can replay a stored one — the
study scores it without re-running the subject.

seed.description: Per-trial seed this transcript was produced with, when set.

trial.description:
0-based trial index for the case this transcript came from (echoed into
the resulting [`RunResult`] so it keeps its trial identity on re-score).

trials.description: Total trials for this case (`0`/`1` = single run).

Source.description:
Where a media [`Part`]'s bytes come from — **exactly one** of a referenced
`uri` (URL or `data:` URI) or inline base64 `data`.

Modelled as an enum, not two `Option`s, so the "exactly one" invariant is
enforced by the type: a media part with *neither* source fails to
deserialize (rather than silently becoming ambiguous), and *both* is
unrepresentable. Serializes externally-tagged: `{"uri":"…"}` / `{"data":"…"}`.

oneOf.description: A URL or `data:` URI the consumer fetches/decodes.

Step.description:
One interaction turn (ATIF _StepObject_): a system prompt, a user message,
or a complete agent turn (inference, tool calls, observation).

is_copied_context.description:
True when this step was copied from a prior trajectory for context
(e.g. retained across a compaction boundary) — not a new interaction.

llm_call_count.description:
Number of LLM inferences this step represents. `Some(0)` on an agent
step marks a deterministic (non-LLM) dispatch; `None` means the
producer didn't track it.

message.description:
The dialogue message. Required by ATIF (it may be an empty string) and
always serialized; parsing is lenient — a step that omits it defaults
to empty text instead of rejecting the whole document.

metrics.description: LLM operational metrics for this step.

model_name.description: The LLM used for this turn, overriding [`Agent::model_name`].

observation.description:
Environment feedback for this step's actions, correlated to
`tool_calls` via `source_call_id`.

reasoning_content.description: The agent's explicit internal reasoning, when surfaced.

reasoning_effort.description:
Qualitative or quantitative effort (`"low"` / `0.3` / …). Carried as
opaque JSON — ATIF allows string or float.

source.description: Who produced this step (open vocabulary — see [`StepSource`]).

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp, carried as an opaque string.

tool_calls.description: Structured tool/function invocations made in this step.

StepContent.description:
A step's `message` (or an observation's `content`): plain text, or — since
ATIF v1.6 — an ordered list of multimodal [`ContentPart`]s. Untagged on the
wire: a JSON string or a JSON array.

StepMetrics.description:
Per-step LLM metrics (ATIF _MetricsSchema_). All optional. Token ids and
logprobs are parsed and round-tripped, never interpreted by Mira.

cached_tokens.description: The subset of `prompt_tokens` served from cache.

completion_token_ids.description: Completion token ids (carried opaquely for RL pipelines).

completion_tokens.description: Tokens generated by the response (reasoning + tool calls included).

cost_usd.description: Monetary cost of this step's API call, in USD.

extra.description:
Provider-specific extras. ATIF has no first-class reasoning-token
field; providers stash it here as `reasoning_tokens`, and the usage
projection reads that key when present.

prompt_token_ids.description: Prompt token ids (carried opaquely for RL pipelines).

prompt_tokens.description: Total input tokens for this turn, **including** cached tokens.

SubagentTrajectoryRef.description:
A reference to a delegated subagent trajectory (ATIF
_SubagentTrajectoryRefSchema_). At least one of `trajectory_id` (embedded
form) or `trajectory_path` (file-ref form) must be set to be resolvable.

extra.description: Custom metadata about the subagent execution (summary, exit status, …).

session_id.description: The subagent's run identity — informational only, never a resolution key.

trajectory_id.description: Resolution key against the parent's `subagent_trajectories` array.

trajectory_path.description: External location of the subagent trajectory (file path, URL, …).

available.description: False when a real provider's API key is absent in the study's env.

metadata.description:
Free-form per-target config that rides the target column: agent, underlying
model, effort, price, sandbox, observability links, … Mirrors
`Target::metadata` onto the wire so the host can surface and group by
it. Defaulted/omitted when empty, so an older study still parses.

provider.description:
Provider id (e.g. `sim`, `anthropic`, `openai`). Lets the host bucket
concurrency per provider so one provider's rate limits can't be flooded.
Defaulted (empty) so an older/foreign study that omits it still parses;
such cases share the empty-provider bucket.

Timing.description:
Wall-clock timing for a run. Subjects that can measure it populate these;
the rest leave them at their defaults.

duration_ms.description: Total wall-clock duration of the run, in milliseconds.

time_to_first_token_ms.description:
Time from run start to the first streamed token/event, in milliseconds,
when the subject can measure it (latency a user perceives first).

arguments.description: The invocation arguments — a JSON object per ATIF (may be `{}`).

function_name.description: The tool/function name (what `tool_called(...)` and friends grade).

Trajectory.description:
One ATIF trajectory document: the root object (RFC 0001, ATIF-v1.7).

Attach it via [`Transcript::from_trajectory`] (or set
[`Transcript::trajectory`] directly): the flat transcript fields are then
derived automatically — see [`Trajectory::project_into`].

agent.description: The agent system that produced this trajectory.

continued_trajectory_ref.description:
Reference to a continuation trajectory file, when context management
split the run across documents.

extra.description:
Custom root-level metadata. Mira never reads or writes `extra.reward`
on the wire (verdicts live in `Score`/`RunResult`).

final_metrics.description: Aggregate token/cost totals for the whole trajectory.

notes.description: Free-form producer notes (design notes, format discrepancies).

schema_version.description:
ATIF compatibility marker, e.g. `"ATIF-v1.7"`. Required by ATIF; Mira
emits [`ATIF_VERSION`] and parses any `ATIF-v1.x`.

session_id.description: Run-scoped identifier (may be shared by a parent and its subagents).

steps.description: The complete interaction history, ordered by `step_id` (1-based).

subagent_trajectories.description:
Embedded subagent trajectories (each a complete, independently valid
ATIF document). Parsed and round-tripped; projections do **not**
recurse into them — subagents are opaque-but-preserved.

trajectory_id.description:
Per-document identifier; required on embedded subagent trajectories,
where it is the resolution key for [`SubagentTrajectoryRef`]s.

Transcript.description:
Normalized result of running a [`Subject`] on one
[`Sample`].

Every subject — in-process, CLI, or a custom integration — produces this same
shape, so scorers and reporting never depend on a subject's internals.

Subjects that can produce a structured record of *what the agent did* set
[`trajectory`](Transcript::trajectory) — the primary structured trajectory
contract (ATIF; see [`crate::trajectory`]). **Provide `trajectory` and the
rest is derived**: the flat fields (`final_response`, `tool_calls`,
`iterations`, `usage`) are its projections, filled automatically by the
framework wherever a transcript is produced or received (see
[`Transcript::project_trajectory`]) — a trajectory-only transcript works
with every existing scorer, no extra calls required. `events` is optional
and fully independent of `trajectory`: providing one, the other, both, or
neither are all valid, with no consistency obligation on the producer.

error.description: Set when the subject failed to complete the run.

error_kind.description:
Classifies `error`: a [`Subject`](ErrorKind::Subject) failure (the model
under test got it wrong — scored as a failure) vs. an
[`Infra`](ErrorKind::Infra) failure (budget, rate limit, provider outage,
timeout — not the model's fault). Defaulted/omitted for the common subject
case; meaningless when `error` is `None`.

events.description:
**Advanced / secondary**: raw, producer-shaped debug events (e.g. the
everruns `Event` JSONL transcript). No cross-subject shape — scorers
and consumers must prefer [`trajectory`](Transcript::trajectory) for
anything it models (tool calls, arguments, observations, metrics);
`events` is only for debugging and data the trajectory doesn't carry.
Optional and independent of `trajectory` (either, both, or neither).

files.description: Files present in the subject's workspace after the run (path → contents).

metadata.description: Free-form metadata: observability links, run ids, etc.

metrics.description:
Extensible **numeric** metrics a subject measured that the core doesn't
model as a typed field (recall@k, energy_joules, p95 latency, …).

Design: `Usage`/`Timing` stay typed because shared budget scorers depend
on their exact shape; everything else is an *open vocabulary* keyed by
name so a subject can report a *new metric key* and grade it with
[`metric_within`]/[`metric_at_least`] without a new protocol version (the
`metrics` map itself is a versioned, additive part of the wire). Use this
(not `metadata`) for anything you want to compare numerically — values
stay `f64`, surface in the JSON/HTML reports, and feed generic scorers.

[`metric_within`]: crate::scorer::metric_within
[`metric_at_least`]: crate::scorer::metric_at_least

output.description:
Multimodal output — the response as an ordered list of typed [`Part`]s
(text, image, audio, file, structured JSON) for subjects whose result
isn't plain text. `final_response` stays the canonical *text* projection
(a text-only scorer keeps working); `output` carries the modalities text
can't. Empty for the common text-only case.

tool_calls.description: Best-effort list of tool names invoked, in order.

trajectory.description:
Structured ATIF trajectory of the run (steps with tool calls,
arguments, observations, per-step metrics) — the **primary structured
trajectory contract**. Optional: subjects that can produce it do;
text-only subjects omit it. When present, `final_response`,
`tool_calls`, `iterations`, and `usage` are projections of it, derived
automatically ([`trajectory::Trajectory::project_into`], applied by the
framework on produce/receive) — a producer sets this field alone and
owes nothing else, `events` included. See [`crate::trajectory`].

TranscriptSummary.description:
Lightweight transcript carried in results and checkpoints (the raw event
stream is omitted to keep the artifact small).

error_kind.description:
Classifies `error` (subject vs. infrastructure). Lets the host retry
infra-errored cases. Defaulted/omitted for the common subject case.

output.description:
The run's multimodal output parts (see
[`Transcript::output`](crate::Transcript::output)), carried in the
lightweight summary so results/checkpoints retain the non-text modalities,
not just `final_response`. Empty for the common text-only case.

Usage.description:
Token / cost accounting, summed across all turns of a run.

Beyond raw input/output tokens, `cache_read_tokens` and `reasoning_tokens`
capture the breakdowns modern providers report; they default to zero for
subjects that don't surface them.

cache_read_tokens.description:
Prompt tokens served from cache (a subset of `input_tokens` for providers
that bill them separately). Zero when not reported.

reasoning_tokens.description:
Reasoning / thinking tokens (a subset of `output_tokens`). Zero when not
reported.

description: Wire schema for the Mira host<->study protocol (newline-delimited JSON over stdio). Protocol version 1.1. Generated from mira::protocol by `mira-schema-gen` (`just schema`); do not edit by hand.
title: Mira Eval Protocol
